frameworks.name: neural population subspace analysis
frameworks.one_line: Population activity decomposed into orthogonal task-epoch subspaces in motor cortex.

frameworks.name: existing motor cortical models (overlapping subspace accounts)
frameworks.one_line: Models predicting shared preparation/movement subspaces are inconsistent with observed orthogonality.

core_question: How does the same motor cortical population implement preparation and movement on short timescales at the population level?
key_assumptions: neural populations can rapidly change the computation they perform
task-related computation is readable from population-level subspaces
single neurons participate in both preparation and movement
manipulations: task epoch contrast: reach preparation vs movement execution
key_findings: preparatory and movement responses occupy exclusive, orthogonal population subspaces
response correlations reorganize completely between preparation and movement
preparatory subspace activity is lawfully related to subsequent movement subspace activity
interpretations: the same population acts as two separate circuits at different times via orthogonal subspaces
preparation and movement are separate but linked computations linked across subspaces
